result:
The spec is fully implemented and the project builds cleanly with 0 warnings and 0 errors, using 2 of the 10 build cycles.

All three missing operations were added to `WindowStats.cs`, alongside the existing `WindowSum` (which is unchanged):

- **`WindowMin(int[], int, int)`** — linear scan seeded with the first window element, so negative values work correctly.
- **`MaxWindowSum(int[], int)`** — computes the first window's sum with `WindowSum`, then slides in O(n) by adding the entering element and subtracting the leaving one. Since the max is seeded from the first window rather than zero, an all-negative array correctly yields a negative result.
- **`CountAbove(int[], int, int)`** — returns `0` up front when `count > values.Length` (no windows), otherwise uses the same sliding-sum technique and counts windows whose sum is strictly greater than the threshold.

The requirements the spec asks to "declare where expressible" are documented in the class XML doc comment, extending the existing convention (C# has no native contract syntax for them). No test files were created and the project file was not modified.
